title: Backpace or delete don't work after creating a new line
author: Lanayx
body: **Describe the bug** Backspace or delete keys don't work after creating new lines with Enter key. **To Reproduce** 1. Go to https://suneditor.com/playground?ts=93 2. Choose editor (the one that says Hello SunEditor!) 3. In the edit field hit Enter key several times 4. Hit Backspace keys several times **Expected behavior** New lines should start disappearing **Actual behavior** Nothing happens, cursor doesn't move **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: Windows - Browser: Firefox - Version: 152.0.5 **Additional context** Initially reproduced in the app, suneditor library version 3.1.4

title: Feature Request: Add an easier way to remove the Page Break component
author: kosuke1208
body: **Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.** After inserting a **Page Break**, it is not very intuitive to remove the created/displayed component. Unlike tables, there is no obvious UI for deleting a Page Break, so users may have trouble figuring out how to remove it. **Describe the solution you'd like** I would like the **Page Break** component to have a deletion UI similar to tables. For example, when the cursor is placed on the **Page Break**, a trash/delete icon could appear, allowing users to remove it easily. **Describe alternatives you've considered** The **Page Break** can currently be removed by editing the HTML directly, **Undo** or using other workarounds, but these methods are not intuitive for most users. **Additional context** I think having a consistent deletion experience across components (such as tables and Page Breaks) would improve the overall editing experience and usability.

title: Create fails with "Cannot read properties of parent node"
author: BJBigler
body:
The bug report:

[SUNEDITOR:E_CREATE_FAIL] Failed to create editor instance. TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'replaceChild')
    at Mi.retainFigureFormat (suneditor.min.js:1:306846)
    at #Bh (suneditor.min.js:1:687670)
    at Object.method (suneditor.min.js:1:678964)
    at suneditor.min.js:1:335667
    at Map.forEach (<anonymous>)
    at Di.applyRetainFormat (suneditor.min.js:1:335564)
    at ni.clean (suneditor.min.js:1:239650)
    at #Gl (suneditor.min.js:1:488285)
    at suneditor.min.js:1:487226
    at Map.forEach (<anonymous>)

Steps to create:

1) My editing interface is located inside a hidden <div> ("display:none"), which in turn  is located in a <dialog> element.
2) I activate the interface by using JS to open the dialog and create the SunEditor interface.

    sunEditor = SUNEDITOR.create(element,{
        plugins: SUNEDITOR.plugins,
        buttonList: [
            ['font', 'fontSize'],
            ['bold', 'underline', 'italic','fontColor'],
            ['table', 'link', 'math'],
            ['outdent', 'indent', 'align', 'list'],
            ['fullScreen', 'showBlocks', 'codeView'],
            ['removeFormat'],
        ],
    });

3) The erroring line above is called once without failure, with the variable "l" having the class "figure.se-flex-component.se-input-component.se-scroll-figure-x".  On the second call, there's a failure, with the variable having class "figure.se-flex-component.se-input-compon..." and the parentNode is null.

I'm not sure what to do from here.

title: [Bug][v.2.47.0] мХДмЭінМ®лУЬ нЩШк≤љмЧРмДЬ лђЄмЮ• м†ЬмЭЉ мХЮмЧРмДЬ мЧФнД∞л•Љ м≥§мЭД к≤љмЪ∞ м§Дл∞ФкњИмЭі лРШл©імДЬ мї§мДЬк∞А мВђлЭЉмІАлКФ нШДмГБ
author: kimjeonghyun2
body:
# [Bug][v2.47.0] мХДмЭінМ®лУЬ нЩШк≤љмЧРмДЬ лђЄмЮ• м†ЬмЭЉ мХЮмЧРмДЬ Enter мЮЕл†• мЛЬ мї§мДЬк∞А мВђлЭЉмІАлКФ нШДмГБ

мХДмЭінМ®лУЬ Chrome лЄМлЭЉмЪ∞м†А нЩШк≤љмЧРмДЬ SunEditor мВђмЪ© м§С, лђЄмЮ•мЭШ м†ЬмЭЉ мХЮмЧР мї§мДЬл•Љ мЬДмєШмЛЬнВ® лТ§ Enter нВ§л•Љ мЮЕл†•нХШл©і м§Дл∞ФкњИмЭА лРШмІАлІМ мї§мДЬк∞А нЩФл©імЧРмДЬ мВђлЭЉмІАлКФ нШДмГБмЭі л∞ЬмГЭнХ©лЛИлЛ§.

## мЮђнШД л∞©л≤Х

1. мХДмЭінМ®лУЬмЧРмДЬ Chrome лЄМлЭЉмЪ∞м†Ал•Љ мЛ§нЦЙнХ©лЛИлЛ§.
2. SunEditorк∞А м†БмЪ©лРЬ нЩФл©і лШРлКФ playgroundмЧР м†СмЖНнХ©лЛИлЛ§.
3. мЧРлФФнД∞мЧР лђЄмЮ•мЭД мЮЕл†•нХ©лЛИлЛ§.
4. мЮЕл†•нХЬ лђЄмЮ•мЭШ м†ЬмЭЉ мХЮмЧР мї§мДЬл•Љ мЬДмєШмЛЬнВµлЛИлЛ§.
5. Enter нВ§л•Љ мЮЕл†•нХ©лЛИлЛ§.
6. м§Дл∞ФкњИмЭА лРШмІАлІМ мї§мДЬк∞А мВђлЭЉмІАлКФ нШДмГБмЭД нЩХмЭЄнХ©лЛИлЛ§.

## кЄ∞лМА лПЩмЮС

лђЄмЮ•мЭШ м†ЬмЭЉ мХЮмЧРмДЬ Enter нВ§л•Љ мЮЕл†•нЦИмЭД лХМ м†ХмГБм†БмЬЉл°Ь м§Дл∞ФкњИмЭі лРШмЦімХЉ нХШл©∞, мї§мДЬк∞А мВђлЭЉмІАмІА мХКк≥† мГИл°Ь мГЭмД±лРЬ м§Д лШРлКФ м†ХмГБ мЬДмєШмЧР мЬ†мІАлРШмЦімХЉ нХ©лЛИлЛ§.

нХДмЪФ мЛЬ мЮђнШД нЩФл©і лШРлКФ лПЩмЮС мШБмГБмЭД м≤®лґАнХ† мШИм†ХмЮЕлЛИлЛ§.

title: [Bug][v3.1.3] Empty editor duplicates wysiwyg container on Enter key press
author: meansc101
body:
## Bug Report **SunEditor Version:** 3.1.3 **Mode:** `classic`, `balloon` **Browser:** Chrome 149.0.7827.53 (arm64) / Safari on iPadOS 18.x, 26.x **OS:** macOS (Chrome), iPadOS 18.x / 26.x --- ### Description When the editor is **completely empty** and the user presses `Enter`, the wysiwyg container element is **duplicated** inside `.se-wrapper` instead of creating a new paragraph. Each subsequent `Enter` press adds another duplicate, resulting in multiple `contenteditable` regions. This is consistently reproducible on **iPadOS (Safari/WebKit)** and intermittently on **PC Chrome**. --- ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Initialize SunEditor in `classic` or `balloon` mode 2. Leave the editor **completely empty** (no content) 3. Click into the editor to focus 4. Press `Enter` --- ### Expected Behavior A new paragraph `


` is created inside the existing wysiwyg container: ```html



вЖР new paragraph created here
``` --- ### Actual Behavior A new `div` element вАФ with the same attributes as the wysiwyg container (`class`, `style`, `contenteditable`) вАФ is inserted as a **sibling** to the original wysiwyg inside `.se-wrapper`. Each `Enter` press adds another duplicate: ```html




``` --- ### Root Cause Analysis Three issues appear to be compounding: **1. `async/await` in `OnKeyDown_wysiwyg` drops `e.preventDefault()` timing** `OnKeyDown_wysiwyg` is an `async` function and calls `await this.$.eventManager.triggerEvent('onKeyDown', ...)` before `e.preventDefault()` is reached for the Enter key handling. The `await` yields execution back to the browser, which then processes `beforeinput(insertParagraph)` natively before the handler resumes. **2. `OnBeforeInput_wysiwyg` does not handle `insertParagraph`** The `beforeinput` handler only checks character count and read-only state. There is no guard for `e.inputType === 'insertParagraph'`, so the browser's native paragraph insertion proceeds unchecked. **3. `enterFormatBreakAtEdge` receives the wysiwyg container as `formatEl`** When the editor is empty, `selectionNode` resolves to the wysiwyg container itself (not a `

` inside it). `enterFormatBreakAtEdge` then calls `dom.utils.copyTagAttributes(newFormat, wysiwyg)`, which copies **all** attributes of the wysiwyg element (`se-wrapper-wysiwyg`, `sun-editor-editable`, `contenteditable="true"`, etc.) onto a new element and inserts it as a sibling inside `.se-wrapper`. **Why iPadOS reproduces more reliably:** On iPadOS WebKit, the soft keyboard may dispatch `keydown` with `isComposing: true` even without active IME composition. This causes `OnKeyDown_wysiwyg` to `return` early (before any `preventDefault` logic), so the entire handler is bypassed and the browser processes `beforeinput(insertParagraph)` unconditionally. --- ### Video https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3bd5f436-727d-4c73-9bf0-f420fa86f7ca --- ### Additional Notes - The bug only triggers when the editor is **completely empty**. Editors with existing content are not affected (cursor resolves to a text node inside `

`, not the wysiwyg container). - No custom `onKeyDown` handler is registered in our usage. --- ### нХЬкµ≠мЦі мЪФмХљ лєИ мЧРлФФнД∞(`classic` / `balloon` л™®лУЬ)мЧРмДЬ `Enter` нВ§л•Љ лИДл•іл©і, мГИ лЛ®лЭљмЭі wysiwyg мї®нЕМмЭілДИ лВілґАмЧР мГЭмД±лРШмІА мХКк≥† `.se-wrapper` мХИмЧР wysiwyg мї®нЕМмЭілДИ мЮРм≤ік∞А л≥µм†ЬлРШлКФ л≤ДкЈЄмЮЕлЛИлЛ§. Enterл•Љ лИДл•Љ лХМлІИлЛ§ л≥µм†Ьк∞А л∞Шл≥µлРШл©∞, PC ChromeмЧРмДЬлПД мЮРм£Љ л∞ЬмГЭнХШмІАлІМ, **iPadOS WebKit нЩШк≤љмЧРмДЬ мЮђнШДмЬ®мЭі нШДм†АнЮИ лЖТмКµлЛИлЛ§.** кЈЉл≥Є мЫРмЭЄмЭА мДЄ к∞АмІАк∞А л≥µнХ©м†БмЬЉл°Ь мЮСмЪ©нХ©лЛИлЛ§. 1. `OnKeyDown_wysiwyg`мЭШ `async/await` кµђм°∞л°Ь мЭЄнХі `e.preventDefault()` нШЄмґЬ м†ДмЧР лЄМлЭЉмЪ∞м†Ак∞А `beforeinput(insertParagraph)`мЭД м≤Шл¶ђнХ® 2. `OnBeforeInput_wysiwyg`мЧР `insertParagraph` м∞®лЛ® л°ЬмІБ мЧЖмЭМ 3. мЧРлФФнД∞к∞А лєДмЦімЮИмЭД лХМ `selectionNode`к∞А wysiwyg мї®нЕМмЭілДИ мЮРм≤іл•Љ к∞Ал¶ђмЉЬ, `enterFormatBreakAtEdge`к∞А wysiwygмЭШ л™®лУ† мЖНмД±мЭД л≥µмВђнХЬ мГИ мЪФмЖМл•Љ `.se-wrapper`мЧР нШХм†Ьл°Ь мВљмЮЕнХ® --- ## Additional Scenario (same root cause) The same issue also occurs when pressing `Enter` on an **empty paragraph (`


`) within existing content** вАФ not just in a fully empty editor. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Initialize SunEditor in `classic` or `balloon` mode 2. Enter content with a blank line in the middle: ``` Hello (blank line вАФ cursor here) World ``` 3. Place the cursor on the blank line 4. Press `Enter` ### Expected Behavior One new blank paragraph is inserted, cursor moves down: ``` Hello (blank) (blank) вЖР cursor moves here World ``` ### Actual Behavior Two extra blank paragraphs are inserted instead of one, and the cursor does not move: ``` Hello (blank) вЖР cursor stays here (blank) (blank) вЖР unexpected extra line World ``` This is reproducible on the official demo at https://suneditor.com/ko as well. ### Root Cause (same as above) The same async timing gap in `OnKeyDown_wysiwyg` causes both: 1. The browser's native `insertParagraph` fires before `e.preventDefault()` 2. SunEditor's own Enter handling **also** runs after the microtask resumes вЖТ Two paragraph insertions instead of one, cursor left at original position ### Additional Video **Note on the attached video:** - **1st Enter** (on the empty `


`): bug occurs вАФ extra blank line inserted and cursor does not advance. - **2nd Enter** (cursor is now inside a content paragraph): works as expected вАФ one blank line inserted and cursor moves down correctly. This suggests the bug only triggers when `selectionNode` resolves to the wysiwyg container itself (which happens on an empty `


`), and not when the cursor is already anchored inside a text node. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d909e7e4-cc58-4ecd-ad2f-3f2db92293db ---

title: [Bug][v3.1.3][iOS] Korean IME Enter key causes consonant leak to next line  (Regression from #1289 fixed in v2.46.0)
author: meansc101
body:
## Environment

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| SunEditor version | 3.1.3 |
| Mode | balloon |
| Device | iPad (iPadOS 18.x or 26.x, physical keyboard attached) |
| Browser | Safari (WebKit) - Reproducible on: official demo https://suneditor.com/ko (v3.1.3) |
| Affected v2 version | Not reproducible (fixed in v2.46.0) |

---

## Bug Description

When typing Korean text ending with an **open syllable (no final consonant / л∞Ымє® мЧЖлКФ мЭМм†И)**  
such as `мХИлЕХнХШмДЄмЪФ` and pressing **Enter**, the vowel character leaks to the beginning  
of the new line.

This is a **regression** вАФ the same bug was reported and fixed in v2 (see #1289, milestone v2.46.0),  
but the fix was not carried over to v3.

---

## Steps to Reproduce

**Reproducible on the official demo:** This bug reproduces directly on the official demo site https://suneditor.com/ko (currently running v3.1.3) вАФ no custom configuration required.

1. Open SunEditor v3 in **balloon mode** on **iOS Safari** (iPad with physical keyboard)
2. Type `мХИлЕХнХШмДЄмЪФ` (Korean text ending with an open syllable вАФ no л∞Ымє®)
3. Press **Enter**
4. Observe the new line

---

## Expected Behavior

Line 1: мХИлЕХнХШмДЄмЪФ
Line 2: (empty, cursor here)

## Actual Behavior

Line 1: мХИлЕХнХШмДЄ
Line 2: мЪФ|  вЖР 'мЪФ' leaked from line 1

---

## Root Cause (investigated)

iOS WebKit Korean IME does **not** fire `compositionstart` / `compositionend` events  
for Korean input. Instead, it uses `deleteContentBackward` + `insertText` patterns  
and keeps the last syllable in an internal IME buffer.

When Enter is pressed on a syllable **without a final consonant** (мЧіл¶∞ мЭМм†И),  
iOS IME is still treating it as "in composition" and moves the character  
to the next line instead of committing it.

This does NOT happen with syllables that **have a final consonant** (лЛЂнЮМ мЭМм†И,  
e.g. `лЛ≠`, `лЕХ`) because iOS naturally finalizes those before processing Enter.

**Note:** Calling `element.blur()` does commit the DOM visually but does NOT  
clear iOS's internal IME buffer вАФ subsequent Backspace still exhibits the ghost buffer behavior.

---

## Related

- Fixes in v2: #1289 (closed, milestone v2.46.0)  
  вЖТ The v3 rewrite appears to have reintroduced this issue.

---

## Video

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/91846262-4035-4ec3-8ba0-2927b9deec4a

---

## нХЬкµ≠мЦі мЪФмХљ

v2мЧРмДЬ мЭімКИ #1289л°Ь нХік≤∞лРШмЧИмЬЉлВШ v3мЧРмДЬ нХілЛє мЭімКИк∞А мЮђл∞ЬнХШк≥† мЮИмКµлЛИлЛ§.
мЭімЧР нХік≤∞ мЪФм≤≠лУЬл¶љлЛИлЛ§.

title: [v3.1.3] Cannot replace existing image inside <a> link вАФ Modal[image] container is null, then silent deletion after workaround
author: ncoevoet
body: ## Environment - **SunEditor version:** 3.1.3 (also reproduces on 3.0.6+) - **Browsers tested:** Firefox 140, Chrome 138 (both reproduce) - **Init style:** ESM dynamic import (`await import('suneditor')`) - **Use case:** Loading saved transactional-email HTML via `value:` so users can edit it. Content contains image logos served as `data:` URIs inside `` anchors. ## Bug Editing an existing inline `` wrapped in `` (loaded as the editor's initial `value`, not inserted via the image plugin) fails in two ways: ### Case 1 вАФ image not wrapped in any `.se-component` Click on the rendered image вЖТ click the "edit" button in the resizing controller вЖТ modal opens but URL field is **empty** (current `src` not populated) вЖТ fill URL вЖТ Submit throws: ``` [SUNEDITOR.Modal[image].warn] Cannot read properties of null (reading 'contains') ``` Origin: `src/plugins/modal/image/index.js` line 779, `container.contains(anchor)`. `container` is null because `Figure.GetContainer` walks up looking for `.se-component` ancestor and finds none (the image is plain HTML, not a managed component). ### Case 2 вАФ image manually wrapped in `` before passing to `value:` No crash, but Submit **silently deletes** the original image without inserting the new one. URL field remains empty when modal opens (existing `src` is not read into the form). ## Reproduction Minimal HTML loaded via `value:`: ```html ``` Reproduction code: ```js const editor = suneditor.create(document.getElementById('editor'), { value: '', plugins: { image: plugins.image }, buttonList: [['image']], }); ``` Steps: 1. Render the editor with the above `value`. 2. Click on the visible image. 3. Click the "edit" button in the resizing controller that appears. 4. Type any new URL into the "Image URL" field. 5. Click Submit. вЖТ Crash (Case 1) or silent delete (Case 2 with manual wrap). ## Expected The image plugin's edit flow should detect and gracefully handle `` tags loaded via `value:` without the plugin's own `

title: Which event is triggered when changing image alignment?
author: syhue
body: Hi, I would like to ask which editor event is expected to be triggered when changing image alignment (left/center/right) in SunEditor. Currently I noticed that the image HTML/classes/styles are updated correctly, but events such as `onChange`, `onInput`, and `onImageAction` do not seem to fire consistently for image alignment changes. ## Version 3.1.3 ## Additional context https://stackblitz.com/edit/gsltmdmx-wpysja2p?file=index.js

title: SunEditor popovers rendered behind Angular CDK overlay (popover / top-layer)
author: syhue
body: **Describe the bug** When using SunEditor inside an application that uses Angular CDK Overlay (v17+ / v21), UI elements such as: - .se-list-layer (dropdowns) - .se-dialog (dialogs) are rendered behind the CDK overlay (e.g., modal/dialog). This occurs even when: - Increasing z-index - Applying position: fixed - Ensuring no conflicting stacking contexts As a result: - SunEditor dropdowns are not visible - Editor becomes partially unusable inside modals **To Reproduce** IвАЩve attached the ZIP file, as the playground on the website is still using Angular 17. [angular_test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27229987/angular_test.zip) Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Click on the image icon in the toolbar. 2. The image dialog appeared behind the CDK. **Expected behavior** SunEditor floating UI (dropdowns, dialogs, popovers) should: - Always render above parent UI containers - Be visible and usable even when the editor is used inside modal/dialog systems (e.g., Angular CDK, Material, etc.) **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: Window - Browser: Chrome **Root Cause** Angular CDK Overlay (in newer versions) uses the native HTML [popover attribute](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Global_attributes/popover), which promotes the overlay into the browserвАЩs top layer. This means: - CDK overlays are rendered outside normal DOM stacking context - They ignore z-index rules from regular DOM elements SunEditor, however: - Does not use a portal/top-layer mechanism рЯСЙ Result: SunEditor UI remains in the normal DOM layer and gets visually blocked by CDK overlays. **Additional context** After upgrading from SunEditor v2 to v3, SunEditor floating UI elements such as .se-list-layer and .se-dialog appear to be rendered outside the Angular CDK overlay context. In our application, the CDK overlay is promoted into the browserвАЩs top layer because it uses the native popover behavior. Elements in the top layer are rendered above normal DOM elements and are not affected by regular z-index ordering. In SunEditor v2, the floating UI elements were rendered within the CDK dialog/overlay context, so they appeared correctly above the modal content. In SunEditor v3, these floating UI elements appear to be rendered outside that CDK overlay context, placing them back in the normal document layer. Because the CDK modal is in the browserвАЩs top layer, SunEditorвАЩs dropdowns/dialogs remain visually behind it, even when increasing z-index or using position: fixed. Therefore, the issue is not only a CSS stacking problem. It is caused by a rendering-layer mismatch: CDK overlay is in the browser top layer, while SunEditorвАЩs floating UI is rendered in the normal DOM layer.

title: Tag "contenteditable" removes on content set via editor.$.html.set(htmlString)
author: QlaVs
body:
Hello! I have a custom div block, example: ```
Custom Tag Example
``` My editor settings: ``` attributeWhitelist: { all: 'contenteditable|class|data-*' }, strictMode: { attrFilter: false, classFilter: false, formatFilter: false, styleFilter: false, tagFilter: false, textStyleTagFilter: false, }, ``` I use insert/set methods with string logic. When my `
` block is insetred via `editor.$.html.insert(htmlString)` everything works perfectly and tag `contenteditable` is on its place. But when I try to insert this block via `editor.$.html.set(htmlString)` tag `contenteditable` is being removed from source and `div` block inserts without it. I have tried with and without `attributeWhitelist` param, also tried changing strictMode settings one by one, but it eventually does not do any changes - OS: Windows - Browser: Firefox: 150.0 - Suneditor version: 3.1.2

title: Export PDF toolbar icon doesn't work
author: electricmessiah
body:
I've tried the Export PDF button on the demo, in my own editor, etc. and it doesn't do anything. Is it bugged? I ran it through AI and it said this, so what's going on with it?

> The plugin requires an apiUrl option pointing to a server endpoint that generates the PDF from HTML. Without it, the constructor warns and the action() bails immediately вАФ nothing happens.
>This is a server-side PDF generation feature вАФ SunEditor POSTs the HTML to apiUrl, expects a blob response with Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="...". There's no client-side fallback built in.
>Options:
>Remove the button вАФ simplest. exportPDF requires infrastructure you don't have.
>Client-side print-to-PDF вАФ replace the button with a window.print() call using a print stylesheet, same as how browsers natively print-to-PDF. No server needed.
>Wire up a server endpoint вАФ add a route that accepts { fileName, htmlContent }, runs headless Chromium/Puppeteer or a library like puppeteer/playwright, returns a PDF blob.
>The print button is already in the toolbar and uses window.print() via viewer.print() вАФ it's essentially the same UX. Do you want to just remove exportPDF from the button list, or implement a client-side fallback?
